title: Biology Mastery Challenge
description: Sharpen your biology skills with this 20-question daily challenge covering anatomy, physiology, cell biology, genetics, and more!

questions.questionText: Which organ in the human body is responsible for filtering blood and producing urine?
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter waste from the blood and produce urine.

questions.questionText: Which organelle is known as the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate ATP, the cell’s main energy currency.

questions.questionText: What term describes a group of organisms of the same species living in the same area?
questions.explanation: A population is a group of the same species living in one area.

questions.questionText: Which scientist is best known for the theory of natural selection?
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin proposed natural selection as a mechanism of evolution.

questions.questionText: What molecule carries genetic instructions in most living organisms?
questions.explanation: DNA contains the genetic code for building proteins.

questions.questionText: Which microorganism is used to make bread rise?
questions.explanation: Yeast ferments sugars and produces carbon dioxide, which causes dough to rise.

questions.questionText: What is the function of mRNA in protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: mRNA carries genetic information from DNA to the ribosome for protein synthesis.

questions.questionText: Which pigment is essential for photosynthesis in plants?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs sunlight and drives the photosynthesis process.

questions.questionText: Which type of muscle is found in the walls of internal organs?
questions.explanation: Smooth muscle lines internal structures such as intestines and blood vessels.

questions.questionText: What is the function of ribosomes in a cell?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are responsible for assembling amino acids into proteins.

rewards.description: Earn 400 points for completing the Biology Mastery Challenge!
